description_long: This is a dark gray, twin-engine military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a mid-mounted straight wing configuration. The wings are broad and slightly angled, each featuring a visible engine nacelle mounted underneath. The tail section consists of a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is enclosed with a green-tinted canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and features multiple window panels. Distinctive markings, including crosses and numbers, are visible on the wings and fuselage, indicating its military origin. The nose is equipped with radar antennae and a tricycle landing gear, with the main gear under the wings and a single nose wheel.
description: A dark gray, twin-engine military airplane with a mid-mounted straight wing, enclosed cockpit, and tricycle landing gear. Markings and radar antennae are visible on the fuselage and nose.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the twin engines under the wings, the single vertical stabilizer, and the broad wingspan.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, radar antennae on the nose, and visible landing gear.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose-mounted radar antennae, cockpit canopy, and both engine nacelles under the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the cockpit, tail section, and landing gear, with military markings on the fuselage and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral, no winglets, engine nacelles mounted under each wing.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units under the wings and a single nose gear, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy, likely single or dual-seat.
